scenario: Beacon Marketplace publishes 11000 messages per day in Camunda 8.8 from an external scheduler. The team must publish a message with a name, a correlationKey value, a variables payload, and a timeToLive so the broker correlates the message to a unique waiting instance over the cluster's REST API surface.
question: Which Camunda 8 REST API endpoint publishes a single message for correlation?

options.text: POST /v2/messages/publication with a JSON body containing name, correlationKey, variables, and timeToLive as fields on the request root
POST /v2/messages/broadcast with a JSON body containing name and variables; the broker fans the broadcast out to every waiting subscription for the message name
POST /v2/signals/publication with a JSON body containing the message name as the signal name; messages are routed as signals on the published name string
POST /zeebe/messages/{name} with a JSON body containing correlationKey and variables; the URL path carries the message name as a path parameter for routing

a.text: Correct. POST /v2/messages/publication accepts name, correlationKey, variables, and timeToLive in the request body for message correlation.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no broadcast endpoint for messages; messages are point-to-point and use the publication endpoint.

c.text: Incorrect. Messages and signals are distinct; signals do not carry correlation keys.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented endpoint carries name in the JSON body, not the URL path, on the /v2/messages/publication route.

explanation: Camunda 8 REST API publishes messages at POST /v2/messages/publication with name, correlationKey, variables, and timeToLive in the body.
